description: 55M with T2DM on metformin, A1C 7.2%, HTN, ASCVD 14%, eGFR 85, no CKD. Simplest 3-guideline diabetes case. ACC/AHA R3 (diabetes statin) preempts USPSTF Grade B (P1) and Grade C (P2). ADA R5 (statin for diabetes) converges with ACC/AHA R3 on moderate-intensity statin. ADA R4 (intensification) fires because A1C 7.2% >= 7% on metformin. ADA R2 (SGLT2i cardiorenal) and R3 (GLP-1 RA CVD) do NOT fire — no ASCVD, no HF, no CKD. KDIGO does not activate (eGFR 85, normal).

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy for diabetes
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA R3 (COR I, LOE A): moderate-intensity statin for adults 40-75 with diabetes. With ASCVD risk 14% (>=7.5%) and HTN, high-intensity is reasonable (COR IIa). ADA R5 (ADA-A) converges: moderate-intensity statin for all diabetic adults 40-75. USPSTF Grade B (ASCVD >=10%) also matched but preempted by ACC/AHA R3 (P1). USPSTF Grade C also preempted (P2).

actions.label: Add second glucose-lowering agent (A1C above target on metformin)
actions.rationale: ADA R4 (ADA-A): A1C 7.2% >= 7% target on metformin monotherapy. Options include SGLT2i, GLP-1 RA, or insulin depending on comorbidity profile. Without ASCVD/HF/CKD, agent choice is driven by glycemic efficacy and weight management preferences.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management for diabetic patient
actions.rationale: BP 134/82 on lisinopril. ADA target <130/80 for diabetes. HTN is a modifiable CVD risk factor and a diabetes-specific statin risk enhancer per ACC/AHA.

actions.label: Continue metformin and diabetes monitoring
actions.rationale: Active T2DM on metformin. eGFR 85 — no dose adjustment needed. Ongoing glycemic monitoring integral to CVD risk management.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend statin based solely on USPSTF guidance
contraindications.rationale: USPSTF Grade B is preempted by ACC/AHA diabetes-specific rec (P1). USPSTF Grade C also preempted (P2). The ACC/AHA rec is more specific to the diabetes population.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend SGLT2i specifically for cardiorenal benefit
contraindications.rationale: ADA R2 requires established ASCVD, HF, or CKD (eGFR <=60 or ACR >=30). Patient has none of these — eGFR 85, no ASCVD, no HF. SGLT2i may be an option under R4 intensification for glycemic control, but not for cardiorenal indication.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend GLP-1 RA specifically for cardiovascular benefit
contraindications.rationale: ADA R3 requires established ASCVD or age >=55 with HTN/dyslipidemia/smoking. Patient is 55 with HTN — borderline eligible for R3 via the high-risk path. However, without established ASCVD, the primary indication is glycemic (R4), not cardiovascular.
